holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is ripping — up 5.4% today and 13.2% over five sessions into $141 — but you have no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor the move, just a triple-witching date 88 days out. A small 8-share position riding a vertical move without an earnings or product event to backstop it is the textbook setup to take some off into strength, then wait for a confirmed earnings date or guide to re-engage.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no company catalyst dated)
INTC.bull: Momentum is one-directional and the tape clearly wants to chase a turnaround narrative with no near-term print to disrupt it.
INTC.bear: A 13% five-day move with no dated catalyst is pure positioning — easy to give back on any foundry/AI headline or options-driven unwind.

NVDA.read: No dated company catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — earnings isn't in your feed and there's no ex-div to lean on, so this is a hold by default. Price action is neutral (down 0.85% on the day, up 1.67% on the week at $208.59), which doesn't force a decision either way. The only thing ahead is triple witching ~88 days out, which is a tape event, not an NVDA-specific window.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro tape event, not NVDA-specific)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle still intact and price is consolidating, not breaking — no reason to pre-empt the next earnings print.
NVDA.bear: No near-term dated catalyst means no defined window; a macro/positioning shock into Sept witching could compress mega-cap multiples without warning.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 4.8% on the day and roughly the same over the week with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings date, no ex-div in the window. Without a print to anchor a window, this is a tape-and-positioning move, and the only thing on the horizon is triple witching ~88 days out, which is a flow event, not a thesis event. Wait for an earnings date to be set before sizing a view; until then the -5% week is information, not a setup.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
ORCL.bull: OCI/AI-backlog story intact; a 5% pullback with no news is the kind of dip that resolves up if the next print reaffirms cloud growth.
ORCL.bear: No dated catalyst means no near-term forcing function to repair the tape, and a 1-day -4.8% with no headline often signals something the tape sees before you do.

NOK.read: NOK ripped +7.1% today but is still -2.2% on the week, so this looks like a one-day squeeze rather than a trend change, and there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor an add or trim. With earnings and ex-div both unscheduled here, the honest move is to let the next confirmed date (typically a late-October Q3 print) come into view before committing either way. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is too far out and too broad to drive this name specifically.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (no company-specific catalyst on calendar)
NOK.bull: One-day +7% on a 174-share position suggests something is waking up — follow-through above today's high would re-open an add window.
NOK.bear: No dated company catalyst means today's pop can fade as fast as it came; -2.2% on the 5-day says the trend isn't fixed yet.

NBIS.read: No dated company catalysts on the sheet for NBIS and no position context (entry, P&L, or current price action) to lean on, so there's no defined add/trim window to play. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching ~88 days out on 2026-09-18, which is a flow event, not an NBIS-specific catalyst. Best use of the next stretch is to pin down the next earnings date and any AI-infra capacity/customer announcements before committing to a stance.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not company-specific)
NBIS.bull: AI infrastructure demand and GPU capacity build-out keep NBIS in the momentum cohort heading into any new customer or capacity disclosure.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI-infra name with no dated catalyst to anchor risk; vulnerable to sentiment unwinds and options-driven volatility into September witching.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share position in KO with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. The -3% week pulls it back toward a more interesting entry zone, but without a defined event to lean on, there's no add/trim window to time here; triple witching on 2026-09-18 is a macro tape event, not a KO-specific driver.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no confirmed KO earnings or ex-div on calendar)
KO.bull: Defensive staple pulling back ~3% on the week into a quiet catalyst window — a deeper flush would set up a cleaner add zone ahead of the next dividend/print.
KO.bear: No dated company catalyst means no forcing function; KO can drift with rates and the dollar, and a 5-share position isn't moving the P&L either way.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a brand-new June 2026 IPO and there are no company-confirmed dated catalysts yet — no earnings date, no dividend, no lockup expiry in the feed. With only 10 shares and nothing dated to trade around, the honest stance is to watch until the first earnings print is scheduled or the IPO lockup window (typically ~180 days, so around Dec 2026) comes into view. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is a market-structure event, not a SPCX-specific catalyst.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no SPCX-specific dated catalyst in feed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarce pure-play exposure to SpaceX as a fresh listing; first earnings and lockup dynamics could drive a re-rating once the calendar fills in.
SPCX.bear: Newly-public name with no earnings track record and an unknown ~Dec 2026 lockup expiry that can pressure the float once insiders are free to sell.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
